name: Care Youth Corporation - Falcon Ridge Ranch dba Rite Of Passage Inc/Falcon Ridge Ranch

findings.finding_text:
The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(a) by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 5/17/2026 and was not reported until 5/19/2026.
This was a repeat noncompliance as noted on 03/22/2024, 12/03/2024, 5/27/2025, 7/29/2025 9/16/2025 & 12/10/2025.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a direct care staff acting in a way that compromised clients' health and safety by neglecting to conduct required supervision checks on clients in the West House. During an inspection, the investigator observed facility video coverage of 06/20/23 from 9:30pm through the morning of 06/21/23 at 7am. The staff failed to conduct 15-minute checks on all clients in their assigned area at least 8 times.

The provider was out of compliance by a direct care staff filling out false/deceptive information on a client supervision document and statement-of-fact. During on and off-site inspections, the investigator reviewed a staff’s statement-of-fact and a client supervision log that documented supervision checks of clients were conducted every 15-minutes. The facility video coverage was reviewed and staff was observed filling out the 15-minute supervision check paperwork and did not conduct at least 8 checks.

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to actively supervise a client with line-of-sight check-ins. During an inspection, documentation and an administrative interview supported that a staff did not conduct line-of-sight checks to ensure a client was in the bed. The client had left the treatment facility at approximately 9:23pm and their absence was not discovered until the following morning at approximately 7am by a client(s).

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member using unnecessary painful stimuli during a necessary physical restraint that caused a client undue pain. During the investigation inspection, an interview disclosed and facility video coverage recorded a staff member that acted outside the scope of their training by hyperextending a client's arm and wrist during a physical restraint.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member failing to comply with Section 26B-2-123 and the provider’s behavior management policy and procedure. Throughout the investigation inspection, interviews were conducted, provider documentation was reviewed, and facility video coverage was observed. The information gathered substantiated that a staff member acted outside the scope of their training, acted in a way that was not trauma-informed, and conducted an unapproved physical restraint technique.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member acting violently towards a client. During the inspection, the information gathered from client and staff interviews and provider documentation substantiated that a staff member initiated and engaged in a physical altercation with a client.

The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(a) by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 5/28/2026 and was not reported until 6/1/2026.
This was a repeat noncompliance as noted on inspection report.

The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(a) by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 5/28/2026 and was not reported until 6/3/2026.
This was a repeat noncompliance as noted on 3/4/2024, 4/3/2024, 12/31/2024, 03/20/2025, & 11/17/2025

The Licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-8(1)(a) by failing to maintain the facility to be free from health hazards. During the inspection, building maintenance and cleanliness issues were observed, including water damage, a mold-like substance, old food left on floors and beds, spoiled food in the refrigerator, a hole in a wall, and a broken stair tread. This is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 7/6/23, 7/9/24, 12/31/24, and 11/20/25. Technical assistance was provided on 9/4/25.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing adequate supervision. During the investigation a client was able to ingest nonedible objects on 6 different occasions. This led to multiple trips to the emergency room for check up. The repeated occurrences demonstrate a behavior that requires a higher level of supervision.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not ensuring that all electrical and furnishings are maintained in operating order and in a clean and safe condition, by not ensuring the appearance and cleanliness of the building and grounds are maintained and by not ensuring each bathroom is properly equipped with a dryer or paper towels. At the time of inspection, a room was observed that did not have any lightbulbs in the overhead lighting, a bedroom had a hole in the wall that did not have an existing work order and multiple bathrooms did not have hand towels, paper towels or a dryer. This was a repeat noncompliance.

name: Pine Ridge Behavioral Health dba Behavioral Health

findings.rule_description: Medication and hazardous items maintenance and storage
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with R501-1-8 by not maintaining hazardous items with consideration of the safety and risk level of the population served. During the investigation, a hazardous item (a lighter) was taken from an unlocked office. This is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 07/09/2024, 05/20/2025 and 07/17/2025.

findings.rule_description: Maintain 1 staff to every 4 client ratio
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision that meets staff to student ratio requirements of 1:4. During an on-site inspection, the licensors observed 2 client groups with staff to student ratios of 1:9 and 1:6. The direct care staff were therapists holding therapy groups and the other direct care staff were supervising clients in other areas on the property.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report two allegations of child abuse by staff to Child Protective Services (CPS). During on and off-site inspections, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member offered and provided vapes to clients, showed clients inappropriate photos on their cell phone, and had inappropriate conversations with clients about their sexual activities and illegal substances. A second staff member was alleged to have had inappropriate sexual conversations with clients, flirted with clients, and touched clients inappropriately. The two  allegations were not reported to CPS by the provider.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report two critical incidents to the Office of Licensing. During on and off-site inspections, multiple interviews disclosed two critical incidents involving client rights concerns. The provider was made aware of these allegations, looked into them, and did not submit critical incident reports to the Office of Licensing.

findings.rule_description: Maintain 1 staff to every 4 client ratio
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision that meets staff to student ratio requirements of 1:4. During an on-site inspection, the licensor observed three client groups with staff to student ratios of 1:11, 2:9, and 1:6.  Multiple interviews and documentation reviewed substantiated staff to student ratio noncompliance throughout the week and a half prior to the inspection..

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance by failing to provide direct supervision for a staff without a conditional or full clearance background screening. During on and off-site inspections, interviews disclosed concerns with a staff member’s behavior and background screening. A background screening check was conducted and substantiated that a staff who required direct supervision was not provided with uninterrupted visual and auditory surveillance. The provider was required to provide direct supervision since October 2022 and the staff has had unsupervised direct access to clients.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report five critical incidents to the Office within one business day. On 08/10/2023, the investigator reviewed five critical incidents that occurred on 08/04/2023, 08/05/2023, 08/05/2023, 08/06/2023, and 08/06/2023 respectively. The provider submitted these critical incident reports to the Office of Licensing on 08/08/2023.

name: Solacium Sunrise, LLC dba Sunrise RTC - The Fields

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing services and supervision commensurate with the behaviors and needs of each client. During an investigation inspection, multiple clients on a line-of-sight supervision protocol were left unsupervised and a staff observed the clients exiting the same bathroom together. On the same date, the clients took an inappropriate photo together. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not immediately reporting allegations of abuse to Child Protective Services. During the investigation, the information gathered from an administrative interview and allied agency documentation substantiated that an abuse allegation was made by a client on 10/04/2024 and the provider reported the allegations to Child Protective Services on 10/10/2024.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 10/2/2024 and was not reported until 10/11/2024.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text:
The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing supervision that was commensurate with a client’s behaviors and needs. During an investigation inspection, a client who was on safety watch obtained and ingested over the counter medication.

This is a repeat noncompliance

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not directly supervising a staff member who did not have a cleared background check. In the facility a staff member who did not have a cleared background check was working with clients unsupervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not performing line of sight check-ins every 15 minutes. During the inspection a client was able to leave the facility and went unnoticed for a period of 30 minutes. This is a repeat noncompliance to rule.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to maintain a staff-to-client ratio of one staff to every four clients. During an additional follow-up investigation inspection, the licensing investigator observed 2 direct care staff supervising 10 clients. The staff members were not able to verify they had conducted 15-minute checks. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to maintain a staff to client ratio of one staff to every four clients. During a follow-up inspection, interviews and provider documentation substantiated that 2 direct care staff were responsible for supervising 10 clients. This is a repeat citation.

name: UHS Of Provo Canyon dba Provo Campus

checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: Heather Guanajuato OL Staff Observing Inspection:

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-5(4) by not protecting a client from  any action that may compromise the health and safety of the client through acts or omissions. During the inspection, the investigator determined that the provider failed to summon emergency medical services for a minor client who was rendered unconscious and sustained severe head and facial injuries during a physical assault. Rather than summoning emergency medical transport, the provider elected to call a non-medical transport company to take the client to the hospital, delaying medical treatment

The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) for failing to ensure that each client has the right to be free from potential harm or acts of violence. During the inspection, the investigator determined that the provider failed to act upon explicit safety concerns, peer threats of violence, and unit transfer requests communicated by a minor client and his legal guardian prior to a severe physical attack. The therapist confirmed these concerns and requests were brought to his attention, but the client was not moved.

The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) for failing to ensure that each client has the right to be free from potential harm or acts of violence. During the inspection, the investigator viewed the video footage which showed a client pour water on another client’s head and slap the top of his head. A staff member attempted to intervene, extending her arm, but was pushed aside. Staff failed to use behavior management training and perform appropriate holds and restraints in order to prevent harm to the client. That staff member called on the radio for assistance while the client lifted the other client up, slammed him onto the floor, and punched him in the face four times rendering him unconscious sustaining severe facial injuries and life threatening head injuries.

findings.rule_description: Provide direct supervision that meets supervision and ratio requirements
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision. In the facility two youth left supervision and engaged in inappropriate sexual contact while unsupervised. This is a repeat citation.

findings.rule_description: Provide direct supervision that meets supervision and ratio requirements
findings.finding_text: The program was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision.  In the facility two youth left supervision and engaged in inappropriate sexual contact while unsupervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by withholding and manipulating  information During on and off-site inspections, licensors reviewed 2 unrelated incident reports, written by staff members, that omitted client physical mistreatment. The licensors conducted multiple interviews, read witness statements, and reviewed information provided by allied agencies that substantiated client physical mistreatment occurred during both incidents.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff mistreating and acting in a way that caused serious physical injury to a client. During on and off-site inspections, multiple interviews disclosed and provider documents reviewed, substantiated that a staff conducted an unnecessary and aggressive physical restraint that resulted in serious harm to the client.
This is a repeat noncompliance issue.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule on two separate occasions by failing to comply with mandatory reporting laws by:
1. Failing to report that a child was physically mistreated and seriously injured by a staff member on 01/19/2022. Youth Health Associates did not report to the applicable investigative agency.
2. Failing to report an incident on or around 10/15/2023 in which a client set fire to an item in a neighbor’s backyard. This incident was not reported to the applicable investigative agency.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a child abuse incident to Child Protective Services (CPS). During the inspection, licensors reviewed the provider report and collaborated with CPS. The evidence substantiated an incident occurred on 01/19/2022 in which a client was physically attacked by a staff member and was seriously injured as a result,.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report or accurately report critical incidents to the Office of Licensing. During off-site inspections, licensors observed 8 critical incident reports that were not reported accurately or at all. These incidents included a client medical emergency as a result of reportedly smoking methamphetamine, an unnecessary and aggressive physical restraint that resulted in serious client injury, a physical restraint in which a staff punched a client in the face multiple times that resulted in client injury, 5 incidents of client sexual misconduct, criminal activity, and an AWOL incident. This is a repeat non-compliance concern.

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text:
The provider was out of compliance with this rule on 2 separate occasions:
1. by failing to submit an application no later than two weeks from the applicant becoming associated with the license. During an off-site inspection, licensors observed the DACS background screening system and noted that Youth Health Associates had not submitted an application for a staff member who worked at the facility.
2. by allowing 5 staff members without eligible background clearances to work without being directly supervised. During on and off-site inspections, licensors verified that the provider allowed 5 current staff, without eligible background clearances, to work without constant audio and visual supervision from a staff with an eligible background clearance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by compromising the health and safety of a client. During an on-site inspection, licensors conducted multiple interviews. Evidence substantiated that on 10/30/2023, a client was made to sit on the floor of the van, without a seatbelt, while a staff member drove the provider’s van during an outing.
